articles.title: Farooq Abdullah on assembly polls: Hope new govts do good for people
articles.description: JKNC president Farooq Abdullah acknowledged the public mandate in the recent assembly elections in five states. He expressed hope that the newly formed governments would do 'good for the people' and urged everyone to take care of the nation.

articles.title: BJP 'robbed' Punjab's Rajya Sabha seats, will take revenge: Kejriwal
articles.description: AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal accused the BJP of 'robbing' Punjab's Rajya Sabha seats after seven AAP MPs defected. He vowed to take revenge in the upcoming February elections. His comments were a response to Raghav Chadha's accusations.

articles.title: ‘Not chattels’: Telangana High Court slams ‘unprecedented’ order banning wife from approaching husband amid divorce plea
articles.description: The application was premised on the grounds that the wife had issues with anger management and displayed psychopathic behaviour and hysteria, the Telangana High Court observed.

articles.description: Thirumavalavan declares Tamil Nadu's endorsement of a coalition government, reflecting a rejection of BJP and AIADMK in 2026 elections.

articles.title: India remains top remittance recipient country in the world with $137 billion: UN
articles.description: "India consistently leads as the top recipient of remittances, followed by Mexico," the World Migration Report 2026, released by the International Organization for Migration (IOM) on Tuesday, said.

articles.title: Punjab CM meets President over ‘betrayal’ by AAP defectors
articles.description: Bhagwant Mann demands recall of seven Rajya Sabha members who switched to BJP; Raghav Chadha hits back, likening AAP to a ‘jilted ex.’

articles.title: Sabarimala PIL papers should have been ‘thrown in dustbin’: Supreme Court
articles.description: Taking strong exception to the foundation of the PIL, the bench said that the court had entertained the petition on the basis of material that did not merit judicial consideration.

articles.title: Flashback: How Victor Banerjee’s 1991 Lok Sabha Run Paved Way For BJP’s Bengal Rise
articles.description: Victor Banerjee was fielded by the BJP from the Calcutta North West Lok Sabha constituency in the 1991 general election.

articles.title: One Vote Upsets DMK Veteran K R Periakaruppan in Tiruppattur Assembly Poll
articles.description: DMK veteran K R Periyakaruppan lost the Tiruppattur Assembly seat by a single vote in one of the closest contests in recent Tamil Nadu elections.

articles.title: Lotus Blooms From Gangotri To Ganga Sagar: BJP Sweeps 206 Seats In Historic 2026 Bengal Landslide!
articles.description: "The Lotus is blooming from Gangotri to Ganga Sagar!" Addressing a sea of supporters at the BJP headquarters, PM Narendra Modi hailed the 2026 mandate as a "new history." With a landslide 205 seats in Bengal and a hat-trick in Assam, the PM delivered an emotional tribute to Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee, declaring his soul finally at peace. From a record 93% voter turnout to the total wipeout of Communist rule across India, Modi promised a "Bhay-Mukt" Bengal focused on safety, employment, and strict action against infiltrators. Witness the moments that redrew India’s political map forever.

articles.title: A New Dawn: Amit Malviya Explains BJP’s Historic 206-Seat Two-Thirds Majority In West Bengal!
articles.description: "A blot on democracy has been wiped clean." In an exclusive conversation with India Today’s Gaurav Sawant, BJP IT Cell Chief Amit Malviya breaks down the "Mission Impossible" victory in West Bengal. With a massive 206-seat mandate, Malviya credits PM Modi’s visionary leadership and the people's rejection of TMC’s "threats and intimidation." Highlighting the lessons learned from 2021, he emphasizes that the state has finally chosen the national development journey over infiltration and lawlessness. Watch the full analysis of how the BJP scripted history to bring a "Saffron Sunrise" to Bengal after 79 years of Independence.

articles.title: 6 Reasons Why Mamata Banerjee Lost Bhabanipur To BJP's Suvendu Adhikari
articles.description: The BJP framed the election as an election against fear (of Trinamool Congress) and that messaging seems to have worked.

articles.title: Mumbai police form SIT to probe racket supplying fake birth certificates to illegal foreigners
articles.description: After an internal BMC report revealed 87,000 irregular record corrections, the Mumbai police SIT will investigate the roles of officials and middlemen in providing fake identities to illegal foreign nationals.

articles.title: Congress wins two seats, CPI(M) one in Bengal polls, but vote share falls
articles.description: The Congress and CPI(M) saw a decline in vote share despite together winning three seats in West Bengal's assembly elections.

articles.title: US warns China over deportations ahead of Trump–Xi talks, threatens tighter visas
articles.description: US warns China of visa curbs over deportation delays ahead of Trump’s Beijing visit; over 100,000 undocumented Chinese nationals remain in the US.

articles.title: The people of Bengal were in a mood for change
articles.description: The people of Bengal were in a mood for change

articles.title: Congress weighs CM choice after UDF’s landslide win in Kerala
articles.description: Kerala witnesses a significant political shift as the Congress-led UDF returns to power after a decade. Discussions are underway to select the new Chief Minister from prominent Congress leaders. The incumbent LDF faces a substantial defeat, prompting introspection. The BJP also secures three seats, marking a breakthrough. Voters have expressed their desire for change.

articles.title: ELECTION SHOCKER: No seats for TMC in key regions including Alipurduar and Purulia
articles.description: The West Bengal election results have shown a major setback for the TMC, with the party failing to win any seats in nine districts including Alipurduar, Kalimpong, Purulia, Bankura, Jhargram, and Jalpaiguri. In several regions, despite multiple seats being available, TMC was completely wiped out and could not open its account. The results mark a significant political shift after 15 years of TMC rule in the state. BJP has celebrated a strong performance, with widespread celebrations reported across several cities in India. BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari made sharp remarks after the results, claiming that TMC lacks ideology and would not survive long after the defeat. He also predicted the party’s collapse within 24 hours and criticized its governance and structure. Overall, the outcome reflects a dramatic political change in West Bengal with BJP gaining major momentum and TMC facing its strongest electoral setback.

articles.title: CM race heats up in Kerala; KC Venugopal camp claims support of 43 MLAs, 3 Independents too
articles.description: According to the KC group, if the chief minister is to be decided purely on the basis of number strength, Venugopal enjoys a clear majority.

articles.title: From ‘Poriborton’ in Bengal, to ‘Badlaav’ in Punjab, BJP seeks to draw a straight line
articles.description: Punjab Congress president Amarinder Singh Raja Warring countered the BJP’s optimism with a different political reading.

articles.title: Next West Bengal CM pick: Suvendu Adhikari, Samik Bhattacharya, Agnimitra Paul emerge frontrunners
articles.description: BJP’s sweeping victory in West Bengal has triggered a leadership race for the next Chief Minister, with Suvendu Adhikari, Samik Bhattacharya and Agnimitra Paul emerging as frontrunners.

articles.title: West Bengal Verdict: Three Key BJP Candidates Win Across Hingalganj, Panihati and Ausgram
articles.description: A strong political wave is seen in West Bengal as three prominent BJP-linked candidates register major victories across key constituencies. Rekha Patra, who became a prominent face during the Sandeshkhali protests, has won from Hingalganj by 5,421 votes. Ratna Debnath, mother of the RG Kar hospital victim, has secured a decisive win in Panihati by 28,836 votes. Meanwhile, Kalita Maji, a grassroots BJP worker and house help from Ausgram, has staged a strong comeback, winning by 12,535 votes.The results highlight a significant shift in voter sentiment, with grassroots candidates and emotionally resonant figures playing a key role in shaping the outcome of the West Bengal Assembly elections.

articles.title: West Bengal Assembly term ends May 6: New government likely to take oath on this date
articles.description: According to the Election Commission schedule, if the electoral process concludes by May 6, it marks the end of the current assembly term, paving the way for next government's formation.

articles.title: Zero gravity: TMC loses it all in 8 districts
articles.description: India News: KOLKATA: BJP has delivered a sweeping blow to TMC across eight districts of Bengal — the scale of the victory underlining not just anti-incumbency, bu.

articles.title: BJP daydreaming, will never assume power in Punjab: Mann
articles.description: The chief minister’s remarks came as the BJP registered a big win in West Bengal and also retained power in Assam, while the NDA made a comeback in Puducherry

articles.description: Trump also urged South Korea to join the US mission against Iran after a South Korean ship was hit by an explosion in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: SIR Effect: 'Ineligible' voters exceed win margins in 25 BJP seats in West Bengal
articles.description: A large number of assembly seats in West Bengal have reported that electors marked ‘ineligible under the Special Intensive Revision (SIR), along with those still under adjudication, exceed the final victory margins.

articles.title: Will continue to work towards strengthening Tamil culture, pride: Amit Shah thanks Tamil Nadu voters, reaffirms NDA's commitment to development
articles.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah and Prime Minister Narendra Modi thanked Tamil Nadu voters for supporting the BJP and NDA. They reaffirmed the central government's commitment to the state's development. Actor-turned-politician Vijay's party, TVK, made a significant debut, winning many seats. This marks a potential shift in Tamil Nadu's political landscape.

articles.title: BJP’s surge in West Bengal, Assam to sharpen focus on Uttar Pradesh ahead of 2027 battle
articles.description: With its cadre energised, the BJP may go for a dual strategy of Hindutva plus development in Uttar Pradesh to consolidate support and pre-empt Opposition attempts to shift the discourse towards caste or other issues

articles.title: Mamatata: Routed Mamata Banerjee fumes at SIRprise in West Bengal
articles.description: Mamata Banerjee's 15-year reign in Bengal has concluded dramatically with a surprising electoral defeat, shattering her hopes for a historic fourth term and undermining a critical pillar of the INDIA alliance. Her relentless drive and progressive welfare initiatives couldn't withstand the dual challenges of anti-incumbency sentiments and rising corruption claims.

articles.title: South Korean-operated vessel ablaze in Strait of Hormuz; Trump says Iran fired at ship
articles.description: A South Korean ship experienced an explosion and fire in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. President Trump stated Iran was responsible for the attack. No casualties were reported. Authorities are investigating the cause of the blaze. The incident occurred as the U.S. initiated an operation to secure shipping lanes. South Korea is reviewing intelligence and considering its response.

articles.title: People rejected TMC’s corruption in West Bengal, says Samajwadi Party national vice-president
articles.description: In unusually blunt public criticism, SP national vice-president Kiranmoy Nanda says while alliance politics at the national level is one thing, governance and corruption are separate issues

articles.title: West Bengal assembly election results: A low five for Trinamool as Kolkata fortress falls
articles.description: Trinamool Congress faced a significant setback in Kolkata, losing its stronghold and several key seats to the BJP. High-profile defeats included senior ministers and the deputy mayor, marking a stark contrast to previous elections. While some victories offered solace, the overall results signal a major shift in the city's political landscape.

articles.description: Jharkhand CM Hemant Soren thanked Assam voters for support, emphasizing the JMM's fight for tribal and minority rights amid a challenging election performance.

articles.title: JMM fails to open an account on electoral debut in Assam
articles.description: JMM failed to win seats in Assam despite campaigning for tea tribes. They received 1.5% of votes, aiming to establish their political presence.

articles.title: Arrest Overrides Transit Relief, No Anticipatory Bail: High Court

articles.title: Vijay's TVK wins 107 seats in TN, falls short of majority by 11
articles.description: Actor Vijay's TVK won 107 seats in a stunning political debut in Tamil Nadu, falling 11 short of a majority. The party, which defeated CM MK Stalin in his own constituency, is now seeking support from smaller parties to form a government.

articles.title: BJP's Suvendu Adhikari secures major win over Mamata in Bhabanipur
articles.description: BJP's Suvendu Adhikari defeated CM Mamata Banerjee in the Bhabanipur assembly seat. Adhikari called it a victory for the people, while PM Modi urged for change over revenge after BJP's stellar performance in West Bengal.

articles.title: BJP ends Mamata's rule in Bengal; people chose 'double-engine govt'
articles.description: Following a historic BJP victory in the 2026 West Bengal polls, MP Minister Vishwas Sarang stated the public embraced PM Modi's vision. BJP secured 206 of 294 seats, ending Mamata Banerjee's 15-year dominance and reshaping eastern politics.

articles.title: 'Regime Of Silence': Ukraine Will Begin Ceasefire Tomorrow Night, Says Zelensky
articles.description: Volodymyr Zelensky announces a ceasefire from midnight May 5 to 6, mirroring Vladimir Putin’s May 8 to 9 truce, aiming for de-escalation despite no formal deal
